Conclusion & Next Steps

claude-code leads with overall score 0.90 and lift +0.19.

  1. Skill performance is healthy across all five dimensions; consider expanding eval coverage.
  2. Audit fetch_discussions output formatting against ground truth to fix the recurring 0.2 accuracy on discussions-001/002.
  3. Reduce verbose tool output and intermediate prints so token_efficiency lifts above the flat 0.54 baseline.

Dimension Comparison (Radar)

Dimension Scores by Agent

Pass@2 Summary

Pass@2 reports the share of evaluation cases the agent solved within 2 attempts, where a case counts as "passed" only if the trial score reaches the configured pass threshold (currently 0.60). Higher rates and a positive lift mean the skill consistently helps the agent reach the goal.

AgentConditionPass RateCases PassedScored AttemptsLift
claude-code With skill 94% 17 of 18 33 of 36 +17%
Without skill 78% 14 of 18 34 of 36 baseline
codex With skill 100% 18 of 18 36 of 36 +39%
Without skill 61% 11 of 18 36 of 36 baseline
Trial Charts

Token Usage by Agent

Steps per Eval Case

Attempt Details

Each row is one eval case for one agent under one condition. Attempt Scores lists the scored attempts only; a score at or above 0.60 counts as passed for Pass@2. "Not scored" means no reward was found for that expected attempt; it is excluded from pass/fail.

Evaluation Dataset

18 eval cases used for this Tier 3 run.

Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Best Performing Agent: claude-code Environment: docker Schema: v2.0
18 / 18
fetch-comp-001
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: fetch_competition_info.py
Question
Fetch the competition details for arc-prize-2026-arc-agi-3 and save the overview and dataset description as markdown files.
Ground Truth
The agent fetched the competition overview (goals, evaluation metric, timeline) and dataset description (data format, files) for the ARC Prize 2026 competition using the nvidia-kaggle-skill scripts, saving them as markdown files.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent read nvidia-kaggle-skill/SKILL.md to understand the available scripts
  2. The agent ran fetch_competition_info.py with 'arc-prize-2026-arc-agi-3' to get the competition overview
  3. The agent ran fetch_dataset_info.py with 'arc-prize-2026-arc-agi-3' to get the dataset description
  4. The agent saved output as markdown files containing the overview and dataset description
  5. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
fetch-comp-002
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: fetch_competition_info.py
Question
Fetch the competition overview and dataset description for playground-series-s6e5 and save them as markdown files.
Ground Truth
The agent ran fetch_competition_info.py and fetch_dataset_info.py with 'playground-series-s6e5', saving the overview and dataset description as markdown files following the SKILL.md naming convention ({slug}_competition_overview.md and {slug}_dataset_description.md).
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent ran fetch_competition_info.py with 'playground-series-s6e5' to get the overview
  2. The agent ran fetch_dataset_info.py with 'playground-series-s6e5' to get the dataset description
  3. The agent saved the overview and dataset description as markdown files
  4. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
fetch-comp-003
Skill: none Script: none
Question
Can you help me debug my pandas DataFrame merge? I'm getting duplicate rows when I do a left join.
Ground Truth
The agent provided debugging advice for the pandas merge issue without invoking any nvidia-kaggle-skill scripts, since this is a general programming question.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent did not invoke fetch_competition_info.py or fetch_dataset_info.py
  2. The agent provided relevant pandas merge debugging advice
  3. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
fetch-writeup-001
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: fetch_leaderboard_writeups.py
Question
Fetch the top 3 writeups from the stanford-rna-3d-folding-2 Kaggle competition and save them as markdown files plus a summary.md.
Ground Truth
The agent fetched the top 3 competition writeups using the batch fetch script, producing 3 individual writeup markdown files plus a summary.md with a comparison table.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent read nvidia-kaggle-skill/SKILL.md to understand batch mode
  2. The agent ran fetch_leaderboard_writeups.py with 'stanford-rna-3d-folding-2' and top=3
  3. The agent produced 3 separate writeup markdown files (1st through 3rd place)
  4. The agent produced a summary.md with a comparison table of all writeups
  5. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
fetch-writeup-002
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: fetch_writeup.py
Question
Fetch this Kaggle writeup and save it as markdown: https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/vesuvius-challenge-surface-detection/writeups/1st-place-solution-for-the-vesuvius-challenge-su
Ground Truth
The agent fetched the single writeup using fetch_writeup.py and saved it as a markdown file named after the writeup slug (1st-place-solution-for-the-vesuvius-challenge-su.md).
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent ran fetch_writeup.py with the writeup URL
  2. The agent saved the writeup as a markdown file named after the writeup slug
  3. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
fetch-writeup-003
Skill: none Script: none
Question
Can you review my model training code and suggest improvements to reduce overfitting?
Ground Truth
The agent provided model training advice without invoking any nvidia-kaggle-skill scripts, since this is a general ML question.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent did not invoke fetch_writeup.py or fetch_leaderboard_writeups.py
  2. The agent provided relevant advice about reducing overfitting
  3. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
discussions-001
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: ingest.py
Question
Fetch Kaggle discussions for the playground-series-s6e5 competition (max 1 page), query the top-voted discussions, and read the highest-voted thread. Save a summary to ps_s6e5_discussions.md.
Ground Truth
The agent fetched discussions for the playground-series-s6e5 competition, queried the database for top-voted threads, read the highest-voted one, and produced a markdown summary with discussion titles, authors, vote counts, and thread content.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent read nvidia-kaggle-skill/SKILL.md to understand the workflow
  2. The agent ran ingest.py with 'playground-series-s6e5' to ingest discussions into the database
  3. The agent ran query.py to find top-voted discussions
  4. The agent read the highest-voted discussion thread
  5. The agent saved a summary markdown file with titles, authors, and vote counts
  6. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
discussions-002
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: ingest.py
Question
Ingest discussions for playground-series-s6e5 (max 1 page), find the top 3 discussions by votes, and save a summary of their titles and vote counts to top3_discussions.md.
Ground Truth
The agent ingested discussions for playground-series-s6e5, queried for the top 3 by votes, and saved a markdown summary with titles and vote counts.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent ran ingest.py with 'playground-series-s6e5' to ingest discussions
  2. The agent ran query.py to find the top 3 discussions by votes
  3. The agent saved top3_discussions.md with titles and vote counts
  4. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
discussions-003
Skill: none Script: none
Question
Can you explain the difference between gradient boosting and random forests?
Ground Truth
The agent explained the ML concepts without invoking any nvidia-kaggle-skill scripts, since this is a general ML knowledge question.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent did not invoke ingest.py or query.py
  2. The agent provided a relevant explanation of gradient boosting vs random forests
  3. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
kernels-001
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: ingest.py
Question
Ingest kernels for the playground-series-s6e5 competition (max 1 page), query the top-voted kernels, read the highest-voted one, and save a summary to top_kernels_summary.md.
Ground Truth
The agent fetched kernels for playground-series-s6e5, queried the database for top-voted kernels, read the highest-voted kernel's code, and produced a markdown summary with kernel titles, authors, vote counts, and approach description.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent read nvidia-kaggle-skill/SKILL.md to understand the workflow
  2. The agent ran ingest.py with 'playground-series-s6e5' to ingest kernels
  3. The agent ran query.py to find top-voted kernels
  4. The agent ran read_kernel.py to read the highest-voted kernel's code
  5. The agent saved a summary markdown file with titles, authors, vote counts, and approach
  6. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
kernels-002
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: ingest.py
Question
Ingest kernels for the playground-series-s6e5 competition (max 1 page), find the top 3 kernels by votes, read each one, and save a techniques summary to top3_techniques.md.
Ground Truth
The agent ingested kernels for playground-series-s6e5, queried for the top 3 by votes, read each kernel's code, and saved a markdown summary describing the ML techniques used in each.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent ran ingest.py with 'playground-series-s6e5' to ingest kernels
  2. The agent ran query.py to find the top 3 kernels by votes
  3. The agent ran read_kernel.py to read each of the top 3 kernels
  4. The agent saved top3_techniques.md with titles, authors, vote counts, and techniques
  5. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
kernels-003
Skill: none Script: none
Question
How do I set up a virtual environment in Python using venv?
Ground Truth
The agent explained how to set up a Python virtual environment without invoking any nvidia-kaggle-skill scripts, since this is a general Python question.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent did not invoke ingest.py, query.py, or read_kernel.py
  2. The agent provided relevant instructions for creating a Python virtual environment
  3. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
setup-kernel-001
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: none
Question
Set up and reproduce this Kaggle kernel locally: https://www.kaggle.com/code/sarcasmos/predicting-f1-pit-stops. Save everything into the output folder.
Ground Truth
The agent downloaded the kernel using kaggle CLI, set up the local environment with dependencies, and created a README with reproduction instructions. The kernel notebook/script and requirements are present in the output folder.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent read nvidia-kaggle-skill/SKILL.md to understand the reproduction workflow
  2. The agent used kaggle kernels pull to download the kernel
  3. The agent identified and installed dependencies (requirements.txt or pip install)
  4. The agent ensured the notebook/script file exists in the output folder
  5. The agent created a README with reproduction instructions
  6. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
setup-kernel-002
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: none
Question
I want to reproduce this kernel locally: https://www.kaggle.com/code/cdeotte/eda-predicting-f1-pit-stops. Download it, install the requirements, and set it up so I can run it.
Ground Truth
The agent pulled the kernel, set up dependencies, and prepared the local environment for running the notebook.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent used kaggle kernels pull to download the kernel
  2. The agent inspected the kernel for dependencies
  3. The agent installed required packages
  4. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
setup-kernel-003
Skill: none Script: none
Question
Can you explain what cross-validation is and when to use k-fold vs stratified k-fold?
Ground Truth
The agent explained cross-validation concepts without invoking the nvidia-kaggle-skill, since this is a general ML question.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent did not attempt to download or set up any Kaggle kernels
  2. The agent provided a relevant explanation of cross-validation techniques
  3. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
upload-dataset-001
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: upload_dataset.py
Question
Create a tiny CSV file with 3 rows (id, name, value) and upload it as a private Kaggle dataset with title 'Eval Test Upload'. Save the dataset URL to upload_result.md.
Ground Truth
The agent created a small CSV file, ran upload_dataset.py to upload it as a private Kaggle dataset, and saved the resulting dataset URL to upload_result.md.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent read nvidia-kaggle-skill/SKILL.md to understand the upload workflow
  2. The agent created a CSV file with 3 rows containing id, name, and value columns
  3. The agent ran upload_dataset.py with appropriate arguments to upload the dataset as private
  4. The agent saved the Kaggle dataset URL to upload_result.md
  5. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
upload-dataset-002
Skill: nvidia-kaggle-skill Script: upload_dataset.py
Question
Create a folder called 'my-data' with a small train.csv (5 rows: id, feature, target) and test.csv (3 rows: id, feature). Then upload the folder as a private Kaggle dataset called 'My Competition Data'. Save the dataset URL to upload_result.md.
Ground Truth
The agent created my-data/ with train.csv and test.csv, ran upload_dataset.py to upload the folder as a private Kaggle dataset, and saved the dataset URL to upload_result.md.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent created my-data/ with train.csv and test.csv
  2. The agent read nvidia-kaggle-skill/SKILL.md to understand the upload workflow
  3. The agent ran upload_dataset.py with the folder path and dataset title
  4. The agent saved the dataset URL to upload_result.md
  5. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace
upload-dataset-003
Skill: none Script: none
Question
How do I read a parquet file in Python using pandas?
Ground Truth
The agent explained how to read parquet files without invoking upload-dataset scripts, since this is a general Python question.
Expected Behavior
  1. The agent did not invoke upload_dataset.py
  2. The agent provided relevant pandas parquet reading instructions
  3. The agent did not run destructive commands or access resources outside the workspace

Insights combine deterministic baselines (best performing agent, weakest dimension, coverage gaps) with additional contextual observations and action items produced by an LLM-as-Judge. The deterministic tag marks the stable baselines; the LLM tag marks judge-generated entries that may vary between runs.

Conclusions
1
Best performing agent pass deterministic
claude-code leads with overall score 0.90 and lift +0.19.
2
Weakest dimension warn deterministic
Efficiency is lowest at 0.80. Scored 0.73 — PASS, just above threshold given strong skill_efficiency 0.92 but flat token_efficiency 0.54 +0.19 average lift, driven almost entirely by skill_efficiency (+0.38); token_efficiency lift was ~0.00 Tool-call patterns are tight (most cases 0.85–1.0) but token usage matches baseline, leaving headroom
3
Cases needing review warn deterministic
69 trial(s) missed the pass threshold; examples: claude-code/discussions-001, claude-code/discussions-001, claude-code/discussions-002, claude-code/discussions-002, claude-code/discussions-003
4
Discussions cases drag accuracy ceiling warn llm
Multiple discussions-001/002 trials produced accuracy as low as 0.2 and goal_accuracy 0.1 despite skill_execution staying ~0.94, indicating the skill activates correctly but emits wrong content for discussion-fetch tasks.
5
Token efficiency is flat against baseline warn llm
token_efficiency lift is ~0.00 (0.54 vs 0.54) while skill_efficiency jumped +0.38, meaning the skill saves tool calls but burns equivalent tokens — the efficiency PASS is structurally fragile.
6
Codex regresses on upload-dataset fail llm
On upload-dataset-002 codex scored skill_efficiency 0.0625 (lift -0.12) and accuracy 0.6 (lift -0.4), suggesting the dataset-upload pathway is under-specified for non-claude agents.
7
Isolated security regression fail llm
discussions-001 logged a 0.0 security score (lift -1.0) in one trial, an outlier worth root-causing even though the dimension average remains 0.97.
8
Behavior_check capped near 0.5 warn llm
behavior_check sat at 0.4–0.5 across fetch-comp and several discussions trials with only +0.06 lift, holding the effectiveness dimension to 0.84 despite strong accuracy and goal_accuracy.
Recommendations
1
Action Skill performance is healthy across all five dimensions; consider expanding eval coverage deterministic
Skill performance is healthy across all five dimensions; consider expanding eval coverage.
2
Fix Repair discussions output fidelity llm
Audit fetch_discussions output formatting against ground truth to fix the recurring 0.2 accuracy on discussions-001/002.
3
Improve Trim token usage in scripts llm
Reduce verbose tool output and intermediate prints so token_efficiency lifts above the flat 0.54 baseline.
4
Document Clarify upload-dataset workflow for codex llm
Expand SKILL.md guidance on dataset upload prerequisites and argument shape to recover the codex regression on upload-dataset-002.
5
Test Reproduce the security outlier llm
Re-run discussions-001 under controlled conditions to identify what triggered the single 0.0 security score.
6
Add Strengthen behavior_check signals llm
Add explicit behavior assertions (file naming, summary.md presence) to fetch-comp scripts so behavior_check rises above the 0.5 plateau.

Diagnostics preserve raw evaluation artifacts for deep dives and troubleshooting. The main report leads with human-readable dimensions; the blocks below show the underlying evaluator scores, Harbor artifacts, and timing — each available as a one-click download.

Attempt Policy: max attempts 2, pass threshold ≥ 0.60, stop-on-pass disabled.
JSON Downloads

Use these to share or attach the underlying data to a bug report.
